prompt: What percentage of US adults are left-handed?
source: Papadatou-Pastou et al., 2020, Psychological Bulletin meta-analysis of 200+ studies across 2.4M individuals: prevalence of left-handedness ≈ 10.6%. Consistent with Hardyck & Petrinovich (1977).
revealExplanation: Base-rate estimation is the foundation of probabilistic reasoning. Most people guess in the 5–20% range, which is the right order of magnitude. The actual prevalence has been stable across cultures and decades, hovering near 10%.
